scope.summary: Makes the connection footprint honest by reporting the dedicated pinned connection and the independently observed stateless pool separately, with documentation of their distinct lifecycle and sizing semantics.
out_of_scope: Credentialed D-lane corroboration against V$SESSION is owned by oraclemcp-20cw3.
Physical-session release and SIGTERM leak remediation are owned by B7c/B7b.

entry_points.name: oracle_connection_info
entry_points.trace: The dispatcher returns the pinned primary as connection and the separately observed stateless pool as stateless_read_connection; a successful pool reports its locked open_count, never a blended estimate. Scoped check: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/var/tmp/oraclemcp-p2-8-session-footprint-20260721/target scripts/swarm_discipline.sh bounded-run --timeout 120 -- cargo test -p oraclemcp --lib connection_info_reports_stateless_read_strategy_when_configured -- --nocapture (1 passed).

entry_points.name: [profiles.pool] configuration
entry_points.trace: README.md and docs/configuration.md state that user work remains on one pinned main session, the stateless pool is separate, and max_size caps only additional stateless connections. Scoped check: CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/var/tmp/oraclemcp-p2-8-session-footprint-20260721/target scripts/swarm_discipline.sh bounded-run --timeout 120 -- cargo test -p oraclemcp --bin oraclemcp healthy_optional_pool_is_installed_and_reported -- --nocapture (1 passed).

limitations: No credentialed D-lane V$SESSION capture was run; the separate follow-up oraclemcp-20cw3 owns that server-side corroboration.
pool_open_connections is the exact local pool open_count at observation time, not an estimate of unrelated database sessions.
follow_ups: oraclemcp-20cw3 verifies the reported pinned-plus-stateless footprint against a scrubbed live D-lane V$SESSION observation.
